hypothesis: During REINVENT4-driven singlet fission optimization, applying fragment-based diversity constraints to overrepresented fragments of size 4–7 non-hydrogen atoms (instead of >8) will reduce the final Morgan fingerprint mean pairwise similarity by at least 0.05 relative to the original Fragment-Based regime without a statistically significant decrease (two-sided t-test, p>0.05) in the final average molecular score across five random seeds.
